records.ety: [L. reprehendere, reprehensum, to hold back, seize, check, blame; pref. re- re- + prehendere to lay hold of. See Prehensile, and cf. Reprisal. ]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Reprehended; p. pr. & vb. n. Reprehending.]

defs: To reprove or reprimand with a view of restraining, checking, or preventing; to make charge of fault against; to disapprove of; to chide; to blame; to censure.
quotes: Aristippus being reprehended of luxury by one that was not rich, for that he gave six crowns for a small fish.
In which satire human vices, ignorance, and errors . . . are severely reprehended.
I nor advise nor reprehend the choice.
